op.name: take_along_axis
op.notes: Take values from input array by matching 1-D index. Cost: numel(output).
op.summary: Take values from the input array by matching 1d index and data slices.

blocks.text: Take values from the input array by matching 1d index and data slices.

This iterates over matching 1d slices oriented along the specified axis in
the index and data arrays, and uses the former to look up values in the
latter. These slices can be different lengths.

Functions returning an index along an axis, like `argsort` and
`argpartition`, produce suitable indices for this function.

desc_blocks.text:
Indices to take along each 1d slice of `arr`. This must match the
dimension of arr, but dimensions Ni and Nj only need to broadcast
against `arr`.

The axis to take 1d slices along. If axis is None, the input array is
treated as if it had first been flattened to 1d, for consistency with
`sort` and `argsort`.

blocks.text:
This is equivalent to (but faster than) the following use of `ndindex` and
`s_`, which sets each of ``ii`` and ``kk`` to a tuple of indices::

Ni, M, Nk = a.shape[:axis], a.shape[axis], a.shape[axis+1:]
    J = indices.shape[axis]  # Need not equal M
    out = np.empty(Ni + (J,) + Nk)
Equivalently, eliminating the inner loop, the last two lines would be::
We can sort either by using sort directly, or argsort and this function

The same works for max and min, if you maintain the trivial dimension
with ``keepdims``:

blocks.code:
>>> np.max(a, axis=1, keepdims=True)
array([[30],
       [60]])
>>> ai = np.argmax(a, axis=1, keepdims=True)
>>> ai
array([[1],
       [0]])
>>> np.take_along_axis(a, ai, axis=1)
array([[30],
       [60]])

blocks.text:
If we want to get the max and min at the same time, we can stack the
indices first

blocks.code:
>>> ai_min = np.argmin(a, axis=1, keepdims=True)
>>> ai_max = np.argmax(a, axis=1, keepdims=True)
>>> ai = np.concatenate([ai_min, ai_max], axis=1)
>>> ai
array([[0, 1],
       [1, 0]])
>>> np.take_along_axis(a, ai, axis=1)
array([[10, 30],
       [40, 60]])

blocks.text: take — Take along an axis, using the same indices for every 1d slice
put_along_axis — Put values into the destination array by matching 1d index and data slices
